text.title: Event Sourcing — Event Definitions
text.summary: Base Event dataclass and domain event hierarchy for an Order aggregate.

rule.statement: Events must be immutable (frozen=True). Once persisted, an event is never modified — it is the source of truth.
rule.rationale: Mutable events invalidate the audit log and break replay: if the stored event differs from what actually happened, replayed state diverges from original state.

rule.statement: Events are granular: one event per state change. Never use a catch-all UpdatedEvent with a full-state payload.
rule.rationale: Granular events make the intent explicit, allow targeted projections to subscribe only to relevant changes, and keep schema evolution manageable.

rule.statement: Name events in past tense describing what happened: OrderCreated, OrderItemAdded, OrderPlaced, OrderShipped, OrderCancelled.
rule.rationale: Past tense signals immutability and distinguishes events (facts) from commands (intentions).

example.code:
from dataclasses import dataclass, field from datetime import datetime from uuid import UUID, uuid4
@dataclass(frozen=True) class Event: """Base class for all domain events.""" event_id: UUID = field(default_factory=uuid4) occurred_at: datetime = field(default_factory=datetime.utcnow) metadata: dict = field(default_factory=dict)

example.note: All events inherit from Event (frozen) and carry only the data that changed. No full-state snapshots in events.

antipattern.description: Mutable event: assigning to event.customer_id after construction.
antipattern.reason: Breaks immutability guarantee. Any code that cached the event has an inconsistent copy, making replay non-deterministic.

antipattern.description: Catch-all event: OrderUpdated with entire_order_data: dict containing all order fields.
antipattern.reason: Loses intent (what specifically changed?), bloats the event log, and makes schema evolution impossible without breaking all existing consumers.

text.reference: Frozen Event base + example event skeleton
